'''Cai Taylor-Wray''' (born 24 February 2006) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|fb}} for the [[Warrington Wolves]] in the [[Betfred Super League]].

He has spent time on loan from [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] at the [[Widnes Vikings]] in the [[RFL Championship]].<ref>[https://www.itsrugby.co.uk/players/cai-taylorwray-61472.html itsrugby profile]</ref>

==Background==
Taylor-Wray was born in [[Leeds, West Yorkshire]], England and grew up in the [[Cross Gates]] suburb in East Leeds.<ref>[https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/an-xiii-of-the-u21s-ready-to-take-super-league-by-storm The 13 young guns who will take Super League by storm in 2025 including Wigan trio]</ref> He is of [[Jamaican people|Jamaican]] and English heritage, and is the son of former [[Jamaica national rugby league team|Jamaica]] international [[Jamaine Wray]].<ref>[https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/next-gen-warrington-wolves-cai-taylor-wray-sam-burgess Next Gen: Meet the Warrington Wolves youngster eligible for Jamaica and hoping to follow in his father’s footsteps]</ref>

He played for [[East Leeds ARLFC]] as a junior.<ref>[https://www.ourfootyteam.com.au/blog/other-footy/international-junior-rugby-league/england-academy-team-announcement/2024/07/06/ England Academy team announcement]</ref>

Taylor-Wray played for [[Yorkshire rugby league team|Yorkshire]] in the [[Rugby League War of the Roses|Origin series]].<ref>[https://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/wolves/wolvesnews/23462095.warrington-wolves-youngsters-named-academy-origin-squads/ Warrington Wolves youngsters named in Academy Origin squads]</ref> He also played for the England Academy side.<ref>[https://www.rugby-league.com/article/62920/england-academy-denied-at-the-death-by-sensational-france--- England Academy denied at the death by sensational France]</ref>

He joined the [[Warrington Wolves|Wolves]] Scholarship system aged 14 and gained an academy contract at 16.<ref>[https://www.totalrl.com/meet-cai-taylor-wray-the-level-headed-warrington-wolves-prospect-ready-to-fill-matt-duftys-boots/ Meet Cai Taylor-Wray, the level-headed Warrington Wolves prospect ready to fill Matt Dufty’s boots]</ref><ref>[https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/ranking-every-super-league-club-by-number-of-academy-products-fielded-in-2024 Ranking every Super League club by number of academy products fielded in 2024]</ref>

==Career==
Taylor-Wray formed a key part of the under 18s side that reached the Academy Grand Final against [[St Helens RLFC|St Helens]] in 2023 and continued to play a key role in the academy and reserve teams in 2024, scoring 17 tries in as many appearances.<ref>[https://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/wolves/wolvesnews/24562918.cai-taylor-wray-signs-long-term-warrington-wolves-deal/#:~:text=He%20formed%20a%20key%20part,tries%20in%20as%20many%20appearances. Cai Taylor-Wray signs long-term Warrington Wolves deal]</ref><ref>[https://www.alloutrugbyleague.co.uk/news/salford-red-devils-loan-warrington-1092308 Salford Red Devils loan in Warrington Wolves outside-back]</ref>

In June 2024 he made his début for the [[Warrington Wolves|Wire]] in the [[Super League]] against the [[Wigan Warriors]].<ref>[https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/warrington-wolves-secure-talented-young-full-back-96590/ Warrington Wolves secure talented young full-back]</ref>

In September 2024 Taylor-Wray signed a three-year contract at the [[Halliwell Jones Stadium]] until the end of the 2027 season with an additional club option for a further 12 months.<ref>[https://www.superleague.co.uk/article/4514/taylor-wray-signs-first-team-wolves-deal Taylor-Wray signs first team Wolves deal]</ref>

He spent March and April 2025 on loan from Warrington at the [[Widnes Vikings]] in the [[RFL Championship]].<ref>[https://widnesvikings.co.uk/cai-taylor-wray-joins-vikings-on-dual-registration/ CAI TAYLOR-WRAY JOINS VIKINGS ON DUAL REGISTRATION]</ref>

Taylor-Wray was given the number 1 shirt by head coach [[Sam Burgess]], with former [[Super League Dream Team#2024|2024 Super League Dream Team]] {{rlp|fb}} [[Matt Dufty]] being forced out of the team.<ref>[https://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/wolves/wolvesnews/25584262.cai-taylor-wray-set-warrington-wolves-number-one-shirt/ Cai Taylor-Wray set for Warrington Wolves number one shirt]</ref>
